content: Just as the educational definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive practice for human learners to improve understanding by reviewing past performance, the original autonomous Reflexion agent framework can be repurposed to ground LLM self-reflection mechanisms in spaced, structured metacognitive routines borrowed from high school experiential learning design. This structured, human-like reflexion routine outperforms current ad-hoc single-step LLM self-reflection by building cumulative, long-term dynamic memory that mimics how humans retain and improve from repeated反思.
